description: Water surrendering to gravity in spectacular display, where liquid becomes mist and roar. It represents the power of letting go, the beauty in descent, and transformation through surrender.
rationale: The word 'luphao' begins with the liquid 'lu' connecting to water. The fricative 'pha' captures the breath and mist of falling water. The vowel hiatus 'a-o' suggests the fall itself - from open release ('a') to the pool below ('o'). The stress on 'pha' emphasizes the transformative moment of the fall.
